verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a cytosolic heat shock protein that translocates to the plasma membrane under stress and in tumor cells. Although it lacks a transmembrane domain, documented surface biotinylation and confocal microscopy confirm that it reaches and embeds in the outer leaflet during heat shock (non-apoptotic) and in cancer cells. Its PM localization and anchorage depend on selective interactions with intracellular phosphatidylserine and phosphoinositides. This qualifies as contextual / dual_localization: HSPA1A has a major cytosolic home but a documented minority PM pool that appears under defined non-baseline conditions (cellular stress and oncogenic states). No documented cancer/cell-death/activation-specific ecto-presentation distinct from baseline stress response; dual localization is the governing mechanism.
